faqs.question: Who can be punished under Section 119 IPC?
faqs.answer: Only a public servant who conceals a criminal design that he is legally required to prevent.

faqs.answer: The public servant may be punished with imprisonment up to 10 years.

faqs.question: Can omission amount to concealment?
faqs.answer: Yes. Deliberate failure to disclose information may constitute concealment.

faqs.question: Why is Section 119 stricter than Section 118?
faqs.answer: Because public servants have a legal duty to prevent offences and protect public safety.

ai_summary: Section 119 IPC punishes a public servant who intentionally conceals, suppresses, encrypts, or falsely represents information regarding a planned offence that he is duty-bound to prevent. Because public servants have a special responsibility to uphold the law, the section prescribes enhanced punishment, including imprisonment up to 10 years where the concealed offence is punishable with death or life imprisonment.

title: Concealing Design to Commit Offence Punishable with Imprisonment
chapter: Chapter V - Of Abetment
section_text: Whoever, intending to facilitate or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby facilitate the commission of an offence punishable with imprisonment, voluntarily conceals, by any act or illegal omission, the existence of a design to commit such offence, or makes any representation which he knows to be false respecting such design, shall, if the offence be committed, be punished with imprisonment of the description provided for the offence, for a term which may extend to one-fourth, and, if the offence be not committed, to one-eighth, of the longest term of such imprisonment, or with such fine as is provided for the offence, or with both.
meaning: Section 120 punishes a person who knowingly conceals information about a planned offence punishable with imprisonment, or provides false information about such a plan, with the intention of facilitating the commission of that offence.
key_points: Applies to offences punishable with imprisonment.
Concealment may be by act or illegal omission.
False representations regarding the criminal plan are punishable.
The accused must intend to facilitate the offence or know that facilitation is likely.
Punishment depends on whether the offence is ultimately committed.
Fine may be imposed along with imprisonment.
The section aims to prevent assistance to criminal activities through concealment.
legal_significance: Section 120 ensures that individuals who knowingly hide criminal plans or mislead authorities regarding offences punishable with imprisonment are held accountable, even if they do not directly participate in the offence itself.
essential_elements: There must be a design or plan to commit an offence.
The offence planned must be punishable with imprisonment.
The accused must conceal the design or make a false representation.
The concealment must be voluntary.
The accused must intend to facilitate the offence or know that facilitation is likely.

criminal_design.requirement: A plan or intention to commit an offence punishable with imprisonment.

concealment.requirement: Voluntary concealment by act or illegal omission.

false_representation.requirement: Knowingly providing false information regarding the criminal design.

if_offence_not_committed.both: Court may impose both imprisonment and fine

A person knowingly gives false information to facilitate an offence.

There was no intention to facilitate the offence.
No offence punishable with imprisonment was involved.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of Section 120 IPC?
faqs.answer: To punish persons who conceal or misrepresent plans relating to offences punishable with imprisonment.

faqs.answer: No. The section applies even if the offence is not committed, though punishment is lower.
