simple_explanation: Attacking or threatening a public servant to stop him from doing his lawful duty is a criminal offence.
objective: To protect public servants from obstruction and violence.
legal_significance: The section safeguards the lawful functioning of public administration.
essential_elements: Assault or criminal force is used.
The victim is a public servant.
The act is connected with official duty.

mens_rea.description: Intent to deter, prevent, or retaliate against a public servant.

examples.scenario: A attacks a police officer to prevent an arrest.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 353 IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both
ai_summary: Section 353 IPC punishes assault or criminal force used to deter a public servant from performing official duties.

title: Assault or Criminal Force to Woman with Intent to Outrage Her Modesty
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Of Criminal Force and Assault
bare_act_text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman intending to outrage, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage, her modesty shall be punished with imprisonment not less than one year but which may extend to five years, and fine.
meaning: Section 354 IPC punishes assault or criminal force intended to outrage a woman's modesty.
simple_explanation: Using force or assaulting a woman in a sexually offensive manner is a criminal offence.
objective: To protect the dignity, modesty, and bodily integrity of women.
legal_significance: It is one of the principal provisions dealing with offences against women.
essential_elements: Assault or criminal force is used.
The victim is a woman.
There is intent or knowledge of outraging her modesty.

mens_rea.description: Intent or knowledge regarding outrage of modesty.

examples.scenario: A forcibly touches a woman with sexual intent.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 354 IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 354 IPC punishes assault or criminal force used against a woman with intent to outrage her modesty.

title: Sexual Harassment and Punishment for Sexual Harassment
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Of Criminal Force and Assault
bare_act_text: A man commits sexual harassment by unwelcome sexual advances, demands for sexual favours, showing pornography against a woman's will, or making sexually coloured remarks.
meaning: Section 354A IPC defines and punishes sexual harassment.
simple_explanation: Unwanted sexual advances, requests for sexual favours, showing pornography, or sexually coloured remarks constitute sexual harassment.
objective: To protect women from sexual harassment and offensive conduct.
legal_significance: The section specifically criminalizes workplace and public sexual harassment.
essential_elements: The victim is a woman.
One of the prohibited acts is committed.
The conduct is unwelcome.

mens_rea.description: Intentional commission of prohibited sexual conduct.

normal_case.imprisonment: Up to 3 years for clauses (i) to (iii)

aggravated_case.imprisonment: Up to 1 year for sexually coloured remarks

examples.scenario: A repeatedly demands sexual favours from a female employee.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 354A IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 354A IPC criminalizes sexual harassment including unwelcome sexual advances, demands for sexual favours, pornography, and sexually coloured remarks.

title: Assault or Use of Criminal Force to Woman with Intent to Disrobe
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Of Criminal Force and Assault
bare_act_text: Any man who assaults or uses criminal force to any woman with the intention of disrobing or compelling her to be naked shall be punished with imprisonment not less than three years but which may extend to seven years, and fine.
meaning: Section 354B IPC punishes assault or criminal force used to disrobe a woman.
simple_explanation: Attempting to remove a woman's clothes or force her to be naked is a serious criminal offence.
objective: To protect women from humiliation and sexual violence.
legal_significance: The section specifically addresses acts intended to strip or disrobe women.
essential_elements: Assault or criminal force is used.
The victim is a woman.
There is intent to disrobe or compel nudity.

mens_rea.description: Specific intention to disrobe or compel nudity.

examples.scenario: A forcibly attempts to remove a woman's clothing in public.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 354B IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 354B IPC punishes assault or criminal force used with intent to disrobe a woman.

title: Voyeurism
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Of Criminal Force and Assault
bare_act_text: Any man who watches, captures, or disseminates the image of a woman engaging in a private act without her consent commits the offence of voyeurism.
meaning: Section 354C IPC punishes voyeurism involving observation or recording of a woman during a private act.
simple_explanation: Secretly watching, photographing, recording, or sharing images of a woman in a private situation is a criminal offence.
objective: To protect the privacy and dignity of women.
legal_significance: The section criminalizes both recording and sharing private images without consent.
essential_elements: The victim is a woman.
She is engaged in a private act.
The accused watches, records, or shares such images without consent.

mens_rea.description: Intentional observation, recording, or dissemination.

normal_case.imprisonment: 1 year to 3 years for first conviction

aggravated_case.imprisonment: 3 years to 7 years for subsequent conviction

examples.scenario: A secretly records a woman in a changing room and shares the video.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 354C IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine
bailable: First offence bailable; subsequent offence non-bailable
ai_summary: Section 354C IPC punishes voyeurism, including secretly watching, recording, or sharing images of a woman engaged in a private act.

title: Stalking
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Of Criminal Force and Assault
bare_act_text: Any man who repeatedly follows, contacts, or monitors a woman despite her clear disinterest, including monitoring her online activities, commits the offence of stalking.
meaning: Section 354D IPC punishes stalking of women in physical or electronic forms.
simple_explanation: Repeatedly following, contacting, or monitoring a woman against her wishes is a criminal offence.
objective: To protect women from harassment and unwanted surveillance.
legal_significance: The section recognizes both physical and cyber stalking.
essential_elements: The victim is a woman.
The accused repeatedly follows, contacts, or monitors her.
There is a clear indication of disinterest.

mens_rea.description: Intentional and repeated conduct despite disinterest.

normal_case.imprisonment: Up to 3 years for first conviction

aggravated_case.imprisonment: Up to 5 years for subsequent conviction

examples.scenario: A repeatedly follows and messages a woman after she clearly rejects contact.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 354D IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years and fine
bailable: First offence bailable; subsequent offence non-bailable
ai_summary: Section 354D IPC punishes stalking, including repeated physical or electronic monitoring of a woman.

title: Assault or Criminal Force with Intent to Dishonour Person
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Of Criminal Force and Assault
bare_act_text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person intending thereby to dishonour that person, otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation, shall be punished with imprisonment up to two years, or fine, or both.
meaning: Section 355 IPC punishes assault or criminal force intended to dishonour a person.
simple_explanation: Using force against someone with the intention of insulting or humiliating them is a criminal offence.
objective: To protect personal dignity and honour.
legal_significance: The offence focuses on the intention to dishonour rather than cause physical injury.
essential_elements: Assault or criminal force is used.
There is intent to dishonour the victim.
The act is not due to grave and sudden provocation.

mens_rea.description: Specific intention to dishonour the victim.

examples.scenario: A publicly slaps B to humiliate him.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 355 IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both
ai_summary: Section 355 IPC punishes assault or criminal force used with intent to dishonour a person.

title: Assault or Criminal Force in Attempt to Commit Theft of Property Carried by a Person
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Of Criminal Force and Assault
bare_act_text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person in attempting to commit theft of property worn or carried by that person shall be punished with imprisonment up to two years, or fine, or both.
meaning: Section 356 IPC punishes assault committed while attempting theft of property carried by a person.
simple_explanation: Using force against someone while trying to steal items they are carrying is a criminal offence.
objective: To prevent violent attempts to commit theft.
legal_significance: The section addresses theft attempts accompanied by assault or criminal force.
essential_elements: Assault or criminal force is used.
The accused attempts theft.
The property is worn or carried by the victim.

mens_rea.description: Intent to commit theft.

examples.scenario: A pushes B while attempting to snatch his chain.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 356 IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine, or both
ai_summary: Section 356 IPC punishes assault or criminal force used while attempting theft of property carried by a person.

title: Assault or Criminal Force in Attempt Wrongfully to Confine a Person
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Of Criminal Force and Assault
bare_act_text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person in attempting wrongfully to confine that person shall be punished with imprisonment up to one year, or fine up to one thousand rupees, or both.
meaning: Section 357 IPC punishes assault used in an attempt to wrongfully confine a person.
simple_explanation: Using force against someone to unlawfully restrict their movement is a criminal offence.
objective: To protect personal liberty from unlawful restraint and confinement.
legal_significance: The offence targets attempts to wrongfully confine accompanied by assault.
essential_elements: Assault or criminal force is used.
There is an attempt to wrongfully confine the victim.

mens_rea.description: Intent to wrongfully confine.

examples.scenario: A forcibly pushes B into a room intending to lock him inside.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 357 IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year, or fine up to ₹1,000, or both
ai_summary: Section 357 IPC punishes assault or criminal force used while attempting wrongful confinement.

title: Assault or Criminal Force on Grave Provocation
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Of Criminal Force and Assault
bare_act_text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person on grave and sudden provocation given by that person shall be punished with simple imprisonment up to one month, or fine up to two hundred rupees, or both.
meaning: Section 358 IPC punishes assault committed under grave and sudden provocation.
simple_explanation: If a person assaults another immediately after serious provocation, the punishment is reduced.
objective: To recognize reduced culpability due to grave and sudden provocation.
legal_significance: The section serves as a mitigating provision compared to Section 352 IPC.
essential_elements: Assault or criminal force is used.
There is grave and sudden provocation.

mens_rea.description: The act is committed under grave and sudden provocation.

examples.scenario: A is gravely provoked by B and immediately pushes him.
examples.result: A may be liable under Section 358 IPC.

max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month, or fine up to ₹200, or both
ai_summary: Section 358 IPC provides reduced punishment for assault or criminal force committed on grave and sudden provocation.

title: Kidnapping
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Kidnapping is of two kinds: kidnapping from India and kidnapping from lawful guardianship.
meaning: Section 359 IPC defines the two categories of kidnapping recognized by law.
simple_explanation: The law recognizes kidnapping from India and kidnapping from lawful guardianship.
objective: To classify the different forms of kidnapping.
legal_significance: This section provides the foundation for Sections 360 and 361 IPC.
essential_elements: Kidnapping must fall into one of the two recognized categories.

mens_rea.description: This is a definitional provision.

examples.scenario: A minor is taken away from lawful guardianship.
examples.result: The act may constitute kidnapping under IPC.

max_punishment: Not applicable
ai_summary: Section 359 IPC defines kidnapping as either kidnapping from India or kidnapping from lawful guardianship.

title: Kidnapping from India
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever conveys any person beyond the limits of India without the consent of that person, or of some person legally authorized to consent on behalf of that person, is said to kidnap that person from India.
meaning: Section 360 IPC defines kidnapping from India.
simple_explanation: Taking a person outside India without lawful consent amounts to kidnapping from India.
objective: To protect individuals from being unlawfully removed from the country.
legal_significance: This is one of the two forms of kidnapping recognized under IPC.
essential_elements: A person is conveyed beyond India's limits.
Consent is absent.
The consent of a legally authorized person is also absent where required.

mens_rea.description: Intentional removal of a person from India without lawful consent.

examples.scenario: A secretly takes B across an international border without B's consent.
examples.result: A commits kidnapping from India under Section 360 IPC.

max_punishment: Not applicable
ai_summary: Section 360 IPC defines kidnapping from India as conveying a person outside India without lawful consent.

title: Kidnapping from Lawful Guardianship
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever takes or entices any minor under sixteen years of age if a male, or under eighteen years of age if a female, or any person of unsound mind, out of the keeping of the lawful guardian without the guardian's consent, is said to kidnap such person from lawful guardianship.
meaning: Section 361 IPC defines kidnapping from lawful guardianship.
simple_explanation: Taking or enticing a minor or a person of unsound mind away from their lawful guardian without consent is kidnapping.
objective: To protect minors and persons of unsound mind from unlawful removal.
legal_significance: Guardian's consent is the key factor in determining the offence.
essential_elements: The victim is a minor or person of unsound mind.
The victim is taken or enticed away.
The lawful guardian's consent is absent.

mens_rea.description: Intentional taking or enticing from lawful guardianship.

examples.scenario: A persuades a 15-year-old boy to leave home without his parents' consent.
examples.result: A commits kidnapping from lawful guardianship.

max_punishment: Not applicable
ai_summary: Section 361 IPC defines kidnapping from lawful guardianship of minors and persons of unsound mind.

title: Abduction
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever by force compels, or by deceitful means induces, any person to go from any place is said to abduct that person.
meaning: Section 362 IPC defines abduction.
simple_explanation: Forcing or tricking a person into moving from one place to another amounts to abduction.
objective: To protect individuals from being forcibly or deceitfully removed from a place.
legal_significance: Abduction itself is not punishable unless committed with a specific criminal intent under later sections.
essential_elements: A person is compelled by force or induced by deceit.
The person moves from one place to another.

mens_rea.description: Use of force or deceitful means is essential.

examples.scenario: A deceives B into entering a vehicle and takes him elsewhere.
examples.result: A abducts B under Section 362 IPC.

max_punishment: Not applicable
ai_summary: Section 362 IPC defines abduction as compelling or inducing a person by force or deceit to move from a place.

title: Punishment for Kidnapping
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever kidnaps any person from India or from lawful guardianship shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years and shall also be liable to fine.
meaning: Section 363 IPC prescribes punishment for kidnapping.
simple_explanation: A person who commits kidnapping from India or lawful guardianship can be punished under this section.
objective: To deter unlawful removal of persons from lawful control or territory.
legal_significance: This is the general punishment provision for kidnapping.
essential_elements: Kidnapping as defined in Section 360 or 361 is committed.

mens_rea.description: Intentional commission of kidnapping.

examples.scenario: A takes a minor away from lawful guardianship without parental consent.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 363 IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 363 IPC punishes kidnapping from India or lawful guardianship.

title: Kidnapping or Maiming a Minor for Purposes of Begging
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever kidnaps or obtains custody of a minor for the purpose of employing or using the minor for begging, or maims a minor for such purpose, commits an offence under this section.
meaning: Section 363A IPC punishes kidnapping, obtaining custody, or maiming a minor for begging.
simple_explanation: Using children for begging or injuring them to make them more effective beggars is a serious criminal offence.
objective: To protect children from exploitation and forced begging.
legal_significance: The section provides severe punishment for organized child exploitation.
essential_elements: The victim is a minor.
The minor is kidnapped, obtained, employed, used, or maimed.
The purpose is begging.

mens_rea.description: Specific intention to use the minor for begging.

aggravated_case.imprisonment: Life imprisonment where the minor is maimed for begging

examples.scenario: A kidnaps a child and forces him to beg on the streets.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 363A IPC.

max_punishment: Life imprisonment and fine
ai_summary: Section 363A IPC punishes kidnapping, obtaining custody, or maiming minors for the purpose of begging.

title: Kidnapping or Abducting in Order to Murder
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
section_type: Offence Relating to Kidnapping and Abduction
bare_act_text: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any person in order that such person may be murdered or may be so disposed of as to be put in danger of being murdered, shall be punished with imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to ten years and shall also be liable to fine.
meaning: Section 364 IPC punishes kidnapping or abduction with the intention that the victim be murdered.
simple_explanation: Taking or abducting someone for the purpose of killing them or exposing them to the risk of being killed is a serious offence.
objective: To prevent kidnapping and abduction committed with murderous intentions.
legal_significance: The section treats kidnapping linked to murder as an aggravated offence.
essential_elements: A person is kidnapped or abducted.
The purpose is murder or exposing the victim to the danger of murder.

mens_rea.description: Specific intention that the victim be murdered or exposed to danger of murder.

aggravated_case.imprisonment: Life imprisonment

examples.scenario: A kidnaps B and takes him to a remote place intending to kill him.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 364 IPC.

max_punishment: Life imprisonment and fine
ai_summary: Section 364 IPC punishes kidnapping or abduction committed for the purpose of murder.

title: Kidnapping for Ransom
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
section_type: Offence Relating to Kidnapping for Ransom
bare_act_text: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any person and threatens to cause death or hurt, or causes apprehension of death or hurt, in order to compel the Government, any foreign State, international organisation, or any person to pay ransom or do any act, commits an offence under this section.
meaning: Section 364A IPC punishes kidnapping for ransom.
simple_explanation: Kidnapping someone and demanding money or forcing someone to do something under threat of harm is a serious offence.
objective: To combat ransom-based kidnappings and terrorism-related hostage situations.
legal_significance: One of the most serious kidnapping offences under IPC.
essential_elements: A person is kidnapped or abducted.
Threat of death or hurt is made or apprehension is created.
The purpose is ransom or coercion.

mens_rea.description: Intent to obtain ransom or compel an act.

aggravated_case.imprisonment: Death penalty

examples.scenario: A kidnaps a businessman and demands ₹50 lakh for his release.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 364A IPC.

max_punishment: Death penalty
ai_summary: Section 364A IPC punishes kidnapping for ransom with life imprisonment or death.

title: Kidnapping or Abducting with Intent Secretly and Wrongfully to Confine Person
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
section_type: Offence Relating to Kidnapping and Abduction
bare_act_text: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any person with intent to cause that person to be secretly and wrongfully confined, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
meaning: Section 365 IPC punishes kidnapping or abduction committed with the intention of secretly and wrongfully confining the victim.
simple_explanation: If a person kidnaps or abducts someone and intends to keep them hidden or illegally confined, he commits an offence under this section.
objective: To prevent kidnapping and abduction carried out for the purpose of unlawful and secret confinement.
legal_significance: The section addresses situations where kidnapping or abduction is used as a means to deprive a person of liberty through secret detention.
essential_elements: A person is kidnapped or abducted.
There is an intention to secretly confine the victim.
The confinement is wrongful or unlawful.

mens_rea.description: Specific intention to secretly and wrongfully confine the victim.

examples.scenario: A abducts B and keeps him hidden in an isolated house to prevent anyone from finding him.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 365 IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 365 IPC punishes kidnapping or abduction committed with the intention of secretly and wrongfully confining the victim.

title: Kidnapping, Abducting or Inducing Woman to Compel Her Marriage, etc.
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any woman with intent that she may be compelled, or knowing it to be likely that she will be compelled, to marry any person against her will, or in order that she may be forced or seduced to illicit intercourse, or knowing it to be likely that she will be forced or seduced to illicit intercourse, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
meaning: Section 366 IPC punishes kidnapping, abduction, or inducement of a woman for forced marriage or illicit intercourse.
simple_explanation: Taking away or forcing a woman for marriage against her will or for sexual exploitation is a serious criminal offence.
objective: To protect women from forced marriage, coercion, and sexual exploitation.
legal_significance: The section safeguards a woman's freedom to choose marriage and protects her from unlawful sexual exploitation.
essential_elements: A woman is kidnapped, abducted, or induced.
The act is done for forced marriage or illicit intercourse.
The accused intends or knows such consequences are likely.

mens_rea.description: Intent to compel marriage or facilitate illicit intercourse, or knowledge that such result is likely.

examples.scenario: A abducts a woman and forces her to marry him against her wishes.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 366 IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 366 IPC punishes kidnapping, abduction, or inducement of a woman for forced marriage or illicit intercourse.

title: Procuration of Minor Girl
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever, by any means whatsoever, induces any minor girl under the age of eighteen years to go from any place or to do any act with intent that such girl may be, or knowing that it is likely that she will be, forced or seduced to illicit intercourse with another person shall be punishable with imprisonment which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
meaning: Section 366A IPC punishes procuration of a minor girl for illicit intercourse.
simple_explanation: Persuading or inducing a girl below 18 years to go somewhere or do something for sexual exploitation is a serious offence.
objective: To protect minor girls from trafficking and sexual exploitation.
legal_significance: The section criminalizes preparatory acts leading to sexual exploitation of minor girls.
essential_elements: The victim is a girl below 18 years of age.
The girl is induced to go from a place or perform an act.
The purpose is illicit intercourse with another person.

mens_rea.description: Intent or knowledge that the girl may be forced or seduced to illicit intercourse.

examples.scenario: A lures a 16-year-old girl to another city intending to hand her over for sexual exploitation.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 366A IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 366A IPC punishes inducing a minor girl for the purpose of illicit intercourse or sexual exploitation.

title: Importation of Girl from Foreign Country
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever imports into India from any country outside India any girl under the age of twenty-one years with intent that she may be, or knowing it to be likely that she will be, forced or seduced to illicit intercourse with another person, shall be punishable with imprisonment which may extend to ten years and shall also be liable to fine.
meaning: Section 366B IPC punishes importing a girl into India for sexual exploitation.
simple_explanation: Bringing a girl under 21 years of age into India for prostitution or illicit intercourse is a criminal offence.
objective: To combat cross-border trafficking and sexual exploitation of young women.
legal_significance: The section addresses international trafficking of girls for immoral purposes.
essential_elements: A girl under 21 years is imported into India.
The importation is from outside India.
The purpose is illicit intercourse or sexual exploitation.

mens_rea.description: Intent or knowledge that the girl will be forced or seduced to illicit intercourse.

examples.scenario: A brings a 19-year-old girl from another country into India for prostitution.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 366B IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 366B IPC punishes importation of girls into India for sexual exploitation or illicit intercourse.

title: Kidnapping or Abducting in Order to Subject Person to Grievous Hurt, Slavery, etc.
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any person in order that such person may be subjected, or may be so disposed of as to be put in danger of being subjected to grievous hurt, or slavery, or to the unnatural lust of any person, or knowing it to be likely that such person will be so subjected or disposed of, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
meaning: Section 367 IPC punishes kidnapping or abduction for grievous hurt, slavery, or sexual abuse.
simple_explanation: Kidnapping someone to torture, enslave, or sexually exploit them is a serious offence.
objective: To prevent severe exploitation and abuse following kidnapping or abduction.
legal_significance: The section criminalizes kidnapping committed for highly exploitative purposes.
essential_elements: A person is kidnapped or abducted.
The purpose is grievous hurt, slavery, or sexual abuse.
The accused intends or knows such consequences are likely.

mens_rea.description: Intent or knowledge regarding grievous hurt, slavery, or abuse.

examples.scenario: A abducts B intending to force him into slavery.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 367 IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 367 IPC punishes kidnapping or abduction for grievous hurt, slavery, or sexual exploitation.

title: Wrongfully Concealing or Keeping in Confinement, Kidnapped or Abducted Person
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever, knowing that any person has been kidnapped or has been abducted, wrongfully conceals or confines such person, shall be punished in the same manner as if he had kidnapped or abducted such person with the same intention or knowledge.
meaning: Section 368 IPC punishes concealment or confinement of kidnapped or abducted persons.
simple_explanation: Helping to hide or unlawfully keep a kidnapped person confined is punishable like the kidnapping itself.
objective: To punish those who assist kidnappers by concealing victims.
legal_significance: The section extends criminal liability to persons who knowingly aid kidnappers after the offence.
essential_elements: A person has been kidnapped or abducted.
The accused knows this fact.
The accused conceals or confines the victim.

mens_rea.description: Knowledge that the victim has been kidnapped or abducted.

normal_case.imprisonment: Same as the corresponding kidnapping offence

examples.scenario: A knowingly shelters a kidnapped child in his house.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 368 IPC.

max_punishment: Same as underlying kidnapping offence
ai_summary: Section 368 IPC punishes knowingly concealing or confining a kidnapped or abducted person.

title: Kidnapping or Abducting Child Under Ten Years with Intent to Steal from Its Person
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any child under the age of ten years with the intention of taking dishonestly any movable property from the person of such child, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
meaning: Section 369 IPC punishes kidnapping or abducting young children for theft.
simple_explanation: Taking away a child below ten years of age in order to steal jewellery, money, or other property from the child is a criminal offence.
objective: To protect young children from theft-motivated kidnapping.
legal_significance: The section specifically targets kidnapping of children for dishonest gain.
essential_elements: The victim is below ten years of age.
The child is kidnapped or abducted.
The purpose is theft of movable property from the child.

mens_rea.description: Dishonest intention to steal property from the child.

examples.scenario: A lures a child away to steal gold ornaments worn by the child.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 369 IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 369 IPC punishes kidnapping or abducting a child under ten years of age with intent to steal property from the child.

title: Trafficking of Person
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever, for the purpose of exploitation, recruits, transports, harbours, transfers, or receives a person or persons by using threats, force, coercion, abduction, fraud, deception, abuse of power, or inducement, commits the offence of trafficking.
meaning: Section 370 IPC criminalizes human trafficking carried out through force, coercion, fraud, deception, abuse of power, or inducement for the purpose of exploitation.
simple_explanation: Recruiting, transporting, sheltering, selling, buying, or receiving people for exploitation such as prostitution, forced labour, slavery, servitude, or organ removal is a serious crime.
objective: To combat human trafficking and protect individuals from exploitation and modern forms of slavery.
legal_significance: Section 370 was substantially amended in 2013 to provide a comprehensive definition of trafficking and enhanced punishments for aggravated forms involving minors, multiple victims, and public servants.
essential_elements: Recruitment, transportation, harbouring, transfer, or receipt of a person.
Use of threats, force, coercion, abduction, fraud, deception, abuse of power, or inducement.
Purpose of exploitation.
Victim's consent is immaterial.

mens_rea.description: The act must be committed for the purpose of exploitation.

aggravated_case.multiple_persons: 10 years to life imprisonment and fine
aggravated_case.minor: 10 years to life imprisonment and fine
aggravated_case.multiple_minors: 14 years to life imprisonment and fine
aggravated_case.repeat_minor_trafficking: Life imprisonment for remainder of natural life and fine
aggravated_case.public_servant_or_police_officer: Life imprisonment for remainder of natural life and fine

examples.scenario: A recruits poor villagers through false job promises and forces them into bonded labour.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 370 IPC.

max_punishment: Life imprisonment for the remainder of natural life and fine
ai_summary: Section 370 IPC comprehensively criminalizes human trafficking for exploitation, including sexual exploitation, slavery, forced labour, servitude, and organ removal, with enhanced punishments for aggravated cases.

title: Exploitation of a Trafficked Person
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever, knowingly or having reason to believe that a person has been trafficked, engages such person for sexual exploitation, commits an offence under this section.
meaning: Section 370A IPC punishes persons who knowingly exploit trafficked persons for sexual purposes.
simple_explanation: A person who knowingly uses a trafficked individual, especially a trafficked minor, for sexual exploitation commits a serious offence.
objective: To punish the demand side of human trafficking and reduce exploitation of trafficked victims.
legal_significance: The section extends criminal liability to those who knowingly engage trafficked persons for sexual exploitation.
essential_elements: The victim is a trafficked person.
The accused knows or has reason to believe that the victim has been trafficked.
The victim is engaged for sexual exploitation.

mens_rea.description: Knowledge or reason to believe that the victim is trafficked.

examples.scenario: A knowingly engages a trafficked minor girl for sexual exploitation.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 370A IPC.

max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 7 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 370A IPC punishes persons who knowingly engage trafficked persons, especially minors, for sexual exploitation.

title: Habitual Dealing in Slaves
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever habitually imports, exports, removes, buys, sells, traffics or deals in slaves, shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term not exceeding ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
meaning: Section 371 IPC punishes habitual involvement in slave trading and slave-related activities.
simple_explanation: Regularly buying, selling, transporting, or dealing in slaves is a grave criminal offence.
objective: To eradicate slavery and punish those engaged in slave trading.
legal_significance: The section targets organized and habitual involvement in slavery-related practices.
essential_elements: The accused habitually deals in slaves.
The conduct includes importing, exporting, buying, selling, trafficking, or removing slaves.

mens_rea.description: Intentional and habitual involvement in slave dealing.

aggravated_case.imprisonment: Life imprisonment

examples.scenario: A runs a network that regularly sells and transports persons as slaves.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 371 IPC.

max_punishment: Life imprisonment and fine
ai_summary: Section 371 IPC punishes habitual dealing in slaves through import, export, sale, purchase, trafficking, or transportation.

title: Selling Minor for Purposes of Prostitution, etc.
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever sells, lets to hire, or otherwise disposes of any person under eighteen years of age with intent that such person shall be employed or used for prostitution, illicit intercourse, or any unlawful and immoral purpose, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to ten years and shall also be liable to fine.
meaning: Section 372 IPC punishes selling or disposing of minors for prostitution or immoral purposes.
simple_explanation: Selling, renting, or handing over a child for prostitution, sexual exploitation, or immoral activities is a serious criminal offence.
objective: To protect minors from commercial sexual exploitation and trafficking.
legal_significance: The section creates a legal presumption against persons who dispose of minor girls to prostitutes or brothel keepers.
essential_elements: The victim is below 18 years of age.
The victim is sold, hired, or disposed of.
The purpose is prostitution, illicit intercourse, or an unlawful and immoral purpose.

mens_rea.description: Intent or knowledge that the minor will be used for prostitution, illicit intercourse, or immoral purposes.

examples.scenario: A sells a 16-year-old girl to a brothel owner.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 372 IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 372 IPC punishes selling or disposing of minors for prostitution, illicit intercourse, or other immoral purposes.

title: Buying Minor for Purposes of Prostitution, etc.
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever buys, hires or otherwise obtains possession of any person under eighteen years of age with intent that such person shall be employed or used for prostitution, illicit intercourse, or any unlawful and immoral purpose, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to ten years and shall also be liable to fine.
meaning: Section 373 IPC punishes buying, hiring, or obtaining minors for prostitution or immoral purposes.
simple_explanation: Purchasing or obtaining a child for prostitution, sexual exploitation, or other immoral purposes is a serious criminal offence.
objective: To punish the demand side of child prostitution and exploitation.
legal_significance: The section complements Section 372 by punishing the buyer or recipient of the minor.
essential_elements: The victim is below 18 years of age.
The victim is bought, hired, or obtained.
The purpose is prostitution, illicit intercourse, or immoral activities.

mens_rea.description: Intent or knowledge that the minor will be used for prostitution or immoral purposes.

examples.scenario: A purchases a minor girl for sexual exploitation.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 373 IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 373 IPC punishes buying, hiring, or obtaining minors for prostitution, illicit intercourse, or immoral purposes.

title: Unlawful Compulsory Labour
chapter: Chapter XIX - Of Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour
bare_act_text: Whoever unlawfully compels any person to labour against the will of that person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
meaning: Section 374 IPC punishes unlawful compulsion of labour.
simple_explanation: Forcing someone to work against their will without legal authority is a criminal offence.
objective: To protect personal liberty and prevent forced labour.
legal_significance: The section reinforces the constitutional prohibition against forced labour and exploitation.
essential_elements: A person is compelled to perform labour.
The labour is against that person's will.
The compulsion is unlawful.

mens_rea.description: Intentional unlawful compulsion of labour.

examples.scenario: A threatens workers and forces them to work without freedom to leave.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 374 IPC.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year, or fine, or both
ai_summary: Section 374 IPC punishes unlawfully compelling any person to labour against their will.

title: Rape
chapter: Chapter XX - Of Sexual Offences
bare_act_text: A man is said to commit rape if he performs any of the acts specified in clauses (a) to (d) under any of the circumstances mentioned in Section 375, including against a woman's will, without her consent, by fear, deception, incapacity, when she is under eighteen years of age, or when she is unable to communicate consent.
meaning: Section 375 IPC defines the offence of rape and specifies the acts and circumstances under which sexual conduct amounts to rape.
simple_explanation: A man commits rape when he engages in specified sexual acts with a woman without valid consent, against her will, through coercion, deception, incapacity, or when she is below eighteen years of age.
objective: To protect women from sexual violence and ensure that sexual acts occur only with free and informed consent.
legal_significance: Section 375 is the primary definition section for rape under IPC. The Criminal Law (Amendment) Act, 2013 expanded its scope beyond traditional sexual intercourse and clarified the legal meaning of consent.
essential_elements: A specified sexual act is committed.
The victim is a woman.
The act occurs under circumstances recognized as rape.
Valid consent is absent, invalid, or legally irrelevant.

mens_rea.description: Intentional commission of the prohibited sexual act under circumstances constituting rape.

examples.scenario: A has sexual intercourse with a woman against her will and without her consent.
examples.result: A commits the offence of rape under Section 375 IPC.

max_punishment: Punishment prescribed under Section 376 IPC
ai_summary: Section 375 IPC defines rape and lays down the circumstances in which consent is absent, invalid, or legally immaterial, forming the basis for punishment under Section 376 IPC.

title: Punishment for Rape
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
bare_act_text: (1) Whoever, except in the cases provided for in sub-section (2), commits rape, shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than ten years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine. (2) Whoever,—(a) being a police officer, commits rape within the limits of the police station to which such police officer is appointed, or in the premises of any station house, or on a woman in such police officer's custody or in the custody of a police officer subordinate to such police officer; or (b) being a public servant, commits rape on a woman in such public servant's custody or in the custody of a public servant subordinate to such public servant; or (c) being a member of the armed forces deployed in an area by the Central or a State Government commits rape in such area; or (d) being on the management or on the staff of a jail, remand home or other place of custody established by or under any law for the time being in force or of a women's or children's institution, commits rape on any inmate of such jail, remand home, place or institution; or (e) being on the management or on the staff of a hospital, commits rape on a woman in that hospital; or (f) being a relative, guardian or teacher of, or a person in a position of trust or authority towards the woman, commits rape on such woman; or (g) commits rape during communal or sectarian violence; or (h) commits rape on a woman knowing her to be pregnant; or (j) commits rape on a woman incapable of giving consent; or (k) being in a position of control or dominance over a woman, commits rape on such woman; or (l) commits rape on a woman suffering from mental or physical disability; or (m) while committing rape causes grievous bodily harm or maims or disfigures or endangers the life of a woman; or (n) commits rape repeatedly on the same woman, shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than ten years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person's natural life, and shall also be liable to fine. (3) Whoever commits rape on a woman under sixteen years of age shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than twenty years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person's natural life, and shall also be liable to fine: Provided that such fine shall be just and reasonable to meet the medical expenses and rehabilitation of the victim: Provided further that any fine imposed under this sub-section shall be paid to the victim.
meaning: Section 376 IPC prescribes the punishment for the offence of rape as defined under Section 375, including enhanced punishment for aggravated categories of rape.
simple_explanation: This section lays down the punishment for rape, with a minimum sentence of 10 years, life imprisonment in aggravated cases (such as rape by police officers, public servants, persons in authority, gang situations, or causing grievous harm), and a minimum of 20 years (extendable to life) where the victim is under 16 years of age.
objective: To prescribe stringent and proportionate punishment for rape, with enhanced minimum sentences for aggravated forms of the offence and for offences against minors.
legal_significance: Establishes a tiered punishment structure for rape based on the severity and circumstances of the offence, with mandatory minimum sentences that courts cannot reduce, and special protection for minors under 16 years of age.
essential_elements: The accused has committed rape as defined under Section 375.
For sub-section (1): the case does not fall under the aggravated categories of sub-section (2).

For sub-section (2): the offence falls within one of the specified aggravated categories (e.g., committed by police officer, public servant, in custody, gang rape, on pregnant or disabled woman, causing grievous harm, etc.).
For sub-section (3): the victim is a woman under sixteen years of age.

mens_rea.description: Intent to commit the act of rape as defined under Section 375, along with knowledge of any aggravating circumstance (such as the victim's age, custodial status, or position of authority) where relevant.

normal_case.imprisonment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 10 years, extendable to imprisonment for life

aggravated_case.imprisonment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 10 years (sub-section 2) or not less than 20 years (sub-section 3, victim under 16), extendable to imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life)

examples.scenario: A police officer commits rape on a woman in his custody at a police station.
examples.result: The officer is liable under Section 376(2)(a) with rigorous imprisonment of not less than 10 years, extendable to life imprisonment, and fine.

examples.scenario: A commits rape on a 14-year-old girl.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 376(3) with rigorous imprisonment not less than 20 years, extendable to life imprisonment, and fine to be paid to the victim.

max_punishment: Imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life), or death in certain aggravated cases under related sections, and fine
ai_summary: Section 376 IPC prescribes punishment for rape, with a minimum of 10 years' rigorous imprisonment (extendable to life) for ordinary cases, enhanced minimum sentences for aggravated categories under sub-section (2), and a minimum of 20 years (extendable to life) where the victim is under 16 years of age.

title: Punishment for Causing Death or Resulting in Persistent Vegetative State of Victim
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
bare_act_text: Whoever, commits an offence punishable under sub-section (1) or sub-section (2) of section 376 and in the course of such commission inflicts an injury which causes the death of the woman or causes the woman to be in a persistent vegetative state, shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than twenty years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person's natural life, or with death.
meaning: Section 376A IPC punishes an offender who, while committing rape under Section 376(1) or 376(2), causes the death of the victim or leaves her in a persistent vegetative state.
simple_explanation: If a person commits rape and in the process causes the woman's death or puts her into a permanent vegetative (unresponsive) state, the punishment is a minimum of 20 years' imprisonment, which may extend to life imprisonment or even the death penalty.
objective: To impose deterrent punishment, including the death penalty, for rape that results in the victim's death or permanent incapacitation.
legal_significance: Creates an aggravated offence linking rape under Section 376 with a resulting fatal or permanently incapacitating injury, allowing for the death penalty as a sentencing option.
essential_elements: The accused has committed an offence punishable under Section 376(1) or 376(2).
In the course of committing such offence, the accused inflicts an injury on the woman.
Such injury causes the death of the woman or causes her to be in a persistent vegetative state.

mens_rea.description: Intent to commit rape, combined with the act of inflicting injury during the commission of the offence that results in death or a persistent vegetative state.

normal_case.imprisonment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 20 years, extendable to imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life), or death

examples.scenario: A commits rape on a woman and, in the course of the assault, inflicts injuries that result in her death.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 376A with rigorous imprisonment of not less than 20 years, extendable to life imprisonment, or the death penalty.

max_punishment: Death
ai_summary: Section 376A IPC punishes a person who, while committing rape under Section 376(1) or (2), inflicts an injury causing the victim's death or persistent vegetative state, with punishment ranging from 20 years' rigorous imprisonment to life imprisonment or death.

title: Punishment for Rape on Woman Under Twelve Years of Age
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
bare_act_text: Whoever, commits rape on a woman under twelve years of age shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than twenty years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person's natural life, and with fine or with death: Provided that such fine shall be just and reasonable to meet the medical expenses and rehabilitation of the victim: Provided further that any fine imposed under this section shall be paid to the victim.
meaning: Section 376AB IPC prescribes an enhanced punishment, including the death penalty, for the offence of rape committed on a girl below twelve years of age.
simple_explanation: If a person commits rape on a girl under 12 years of age, the punishment is a minimum of 20 years' imprisonment, extendable to life imprisonment, along with a fine for the victim's medical and rehabilitation expenses, or the death penalty.
objective: To provide the strongest deterrent, including capital punishment, against the sexual assault of very young children.
legal_significance: Introduces the death penalty as a sentencing option for rape of girls under 12 years and mandates that any fine imposed be used for the victim's medical treatment and rehabilitation.
essential_elements: The victim is a woman (girl) under twelve years of age.
The accused has committed rape on such victim as defined under Section 375.

mens_rea.description: Intent or knowledge in committing the act of rape on a girl who is under twelve years of age.

normal_case.imprisonment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 20 years, extendable to imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life)

aggravated_case.imprisonment: Death

examples.scenario: A commits rape on a 10-year-old girl.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 376AB with rigorous imprisonment of not less than 20 years, extendable to life imprisonment, along with fine for the victim's medical expenses and rehabilitation, or death.

max_punishment: Death
ai_summary: Section 376AB IPC prescribes enhanced punishment for rape on a girl under twelve years of age, ranging from a minimum of 20 years' rigorous imprisonment to life imprisonment or death, with fine to be paid to the victim for medical expenses and rehabilitation.

title: Sexual Intercourse by Husband Upon His Wife During Separation
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
bare_act_text: Whoever has sexual intercourse with his own wife, who is living separately, whether under a decree of separation or otherwise, without her consent, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than two years but which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine. Explanation.—In this section, "sexual intercourse" shall mean any of the acts mentioned in clauses (a) to (d) of section 375.
meaning: Section 376B IPC punishes a husband who has sexual intercourse with his wife without her consent while they are living separately, whether under a decree of separation or otherwise.
simple_explanation: If a husband and wife are living apart (whether by a court order of separation or by mutual arrangement), and the husband has sexual intercourse with her without her consent, he can be punished with imprisonment of 2 to 7 years along with a fine.
objective: To protect the bodily autonomy and consent of a wife who is living separately from her husband, recognizing that marriage does not entitle a husband to non-consensual intercourse during such separation.
legal_significance: Carves out a distinct offence (separate from the marital rape exception under Section 375) where the husband and wife are living separately, making non-consensual intercourse in such circumstances punishable, though with a lower minimum sentence than rape under Section 376.
essential_elements: The accused is the husband of the woman.
The wife is living separately from the husband, whether under a decree of separation or otherwise.
The husband has sexual intercourse (as defined in clauses (a) to (d) of Section 375) with the wife.
Such intercourse is without the wife's consent.

mens_rea.description: Knowledge that the wife is living separately and that she has not consented to the sexual intercourse.

normal_case.imprisonment: Not less than 2 years, extendable to 7 years

examples.scenario: A husband, whose wife is living separately under a decree of judicial separation, has sexual intercourse with her without her consent.
examples.result: The husband is liable under Section 376B with imprisonment of 2 to 7 years and fine.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 376B IPC punishes a husband for non-consensual sexual intercourse with his wife while they are living separately, with imprisonment ranging from 2 to 7 years along with fine.

title: Sexual Intercourse by a Person in Authority
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
bare_act_text: Whoever, being—(a) in a position of authority or in a fiduciary relationship; or (b) a public servant; or (c) superintendent or manager of a jail, remand home or other place of custody established by or under any law for the time being in force, or a women's or children's institution; or (d) on the management of a hospital or being on the staff of a hospital, abuses such position or fiduciary relationship to induce or seduce any woman either in his custody or under his charge or present in the premises to have sexual intercourse with him, such sexual intercourse not amounting to the offence of rape, shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than five years, but which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine. Explanation 1.—In this section, "sexual intercourse" shall mean any of the acts mentioned in clauses (a) to (d) of section 375. Explanation 2.—For the purposes of this section, Explanation 1 to section 375 shall also be applicable. Explanation 3.—"Superintendent", in relation to a jail, remand home or other place of custody or a women's or children's institution, includes a person holding any other office in such jail, remand home, place or institution by virtue of which such person can exercise any authority or control over its inmates. Explanation 4.—The expressions "hospital" and "women's or children's institution" shall respectively have the same meaning as in Explanation to sub-section (2) of section 376.
meaning: Section 376C IPC punishes a person in a position of authority, fiduciary relationship, or custodial control who abuses that position to induce or seduce a woman under his charge into sexual intercourse, where such intercourse does not amount to rape.
simple_explanation: If someone in a position of power, trust, or authority—such as a public servant, jail superintendent, or hospital staff—misuses that position to get a woman in their custody or charge to have sexual intercourse with them (with apparent consent, so it isn't rape), they can be punished with 5 to 10 years' imprisonment and a fine.
objective: To penalize exploitation of positions of authority or trust to obtain sexual intercourse from women in custody or under one's care, where the intercourse, though technically consented to, results from abuse of power.
legal_significance: Addresses situations of constructive coercion arising from an imbalance of power between custodians/authorities and women under their charge, treating such acts as a distinct offence from rape due to the absence of force or lack of consent in the traditional sense.
essential_elements: The accused holds a position of authority, fiduciary relationship, is a public servant, superintendent/manager of a custodial institution, or is on the management/staff of a hospital.
The accused abuses such position or fiduciary relationship.
The accused induces or seduces a woman in his custody, under his charge, or present in the premises to have sexual intercourse with him.
Such sexual intercourse does not amount to the offence of rape.

mens_rea.description: Intent to abuse one's position of authority or trust to induce or seduce the woman into sexual intercourse.

normal_case.imprisonment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 5 years, extendable to 10 years

examples.scenario: A jail superintendent abuses his position to induce a woman inmate under his custody to have sexual intercourse with him.
examples.result: The superintendent is liable under Section 376C with rigorous imprisonment of 5 to 10 years and fine.

max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine
ai_summary: Section 376C IPC punishes persons in positions of authority, trust, or custodial control who abuse that position to induce or seduce a woman under their charge into sexual intercourse not amounting to rape, with rigorous imprisonment of 5 to 10 years and fine.

title: Gang Rape
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
bare_act_text: Where a woman is raped by one or more persons constituting a group or acting in furtherance of a common intention, each of those persons shall be deemed to have committed the offence of rape and shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than twenty years, but which may extend to life which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person's natural life, and with fine: Provided that such fine shall be just and reasonable to meet the medical expenses and rehabilitation of the victim: Provided further that any fine imposed under this section shall be paid to the victim.
meaning: Section 376D IPC punishes gang rape, deeming each member of a group that rapes a woman, or acts in furtherance of a common intention to do so, to have committed the offence of rape individually.
simple_explanation: If a woman is raped by a group of people, or by persons acting together with a shared intention, every person involved is treated as having committed rape and is punished with a minimum of 20 years' imprisonment, extendable to life, along with a fine for the victim's medical expenses and rehabilitation.
objective: To ensure that all participants in a gang rape are held individually and equally liable, with severe minimum punishment, recognizing the heightened brutality and trauma of group sexual assault.
legal_significance: Creates a deeming provision of constructive liability whereby every member of the group is deemed to have committed rape, regardless of which individual(s) physically committed the act, and mandates that any fine be paid directly to the victim.
essential_elements: The victim is a woman who has been raped.
The rape is committed by one or more persons constituting a group, or by persons acting in furtherance of a common intention.
Each person in such group or acting with common intention is deemed to have committed the offence of rape.

mens_rea.description: Common intention shared among the group members to commit rape, or participation as part of a group committing the rape.

normal_case.imprisonment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 20 years, extendable to imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life)

examples.scenario: A group of four men rape a woman, acting together in furtherance of a common intention.
examples.result: Each of the four men is deemed to have committed rape and is liable under Section 376D with rigorous imprisonment of not less than 20 years, extendable to life imprisonment, and fine payable to the victim.

max_punishment: Imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life) and fine
ai_summary: Section 376D IPC punishes gang rape by deeming every member of a group, or persons acting in furtherance of a common intention, to have individually committed rape, with rigorous imprisonment of not less than 20 years extendable to life, along with fine payable to the victim.

title: Punishment for Gang Rape on Woman Under Sixteen Years of Age
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
bare_act_text: Where a woman under sixteen years of age is raped by one or more persons constituting a group or acting in furtherance of a common intention, each of those persons shall be deemed to have committed the offence of rape and shall be punished with imprisonment for life, which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person's natural life, and with fine: Provided that such fine shall be just and reasonable to meet the medical expenses and rehabilitation of the victim: Provided further that any fine imposed under this section shall be paid to the victim.
meaning: Section 376DA IPC punishes gang rape committed on a girl under sixteen years of age, deeming each member of the group to have committed rape and prescribing mandatory life imprisonment.
simple_explanation: If a girl under 16 years of age is gang raped by a group of persons acting together or with a common intention, every person involved is deemed to have committed rape and must be punished with imprisonment for the remainder of their natural life, along with a fine for the victim's medical expenses and rehabilitation.
objective: To provide an enhanced mandatory life sentence for gang rape where the victim is a minor under sixteen years of age, reflecting the heightened severity of such offences against children.
legal_significance: Removes any sentencing discretion below life imprisonment for gang rape of minors under 16, mandating imprisonment for the remainder of natural life for every participant, with fine payable to the victim.
essential_elements: The victim is a woman under sixteen years of age.
The rape is committed by one or more persons constituting a group or acting in furtherance of a common intention.
Each such person is deemed to have committed the offence of rape.

mens_rea.description: Common intention shared among group members to commit rape, or participation as part of the group committing rape on a minor under sixteen.

normal_case.imprisonment: Imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life)

examples.scenario: A group of three men rape a 15-year-old girl acting in furtherance of a common intention.
examples.result: Each of the three men is deemed to have committed rape and is liable under Section 376DA with mandatory imprisonment for the remainder of his natural life, and fine payable to the victim.

max_punishment: Imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life) and fine
ai_summary: Section 376DA IPC mandates imprisonment for the remainder of natural life and fine for every person involved in the gang rape of a girl under sixteen years of age, with fine to be paid to the victim for medical expenses and rehabilitation.

title: Punishment for Gang Rape on Woman Under Twelve Years of Age
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
bare_act_text: Where a woman under twelve years of age is raped by one or more persons constituting a group or acting in furtherance of a common intention, each of those persons shall be deemed to have committed the offence of rape and shall be punished with imprisonment for life, which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person's natural life, and with fine, or with death: Provided that such fine shall be just and reasonable to meet the medical expenses and rehabilitation of the victim: Provided further that any fine imposed under this section shall be paid to the victim.
meaning: Section 376DB IPC punishes gang rape committed on a girl under twelve years of age, deeming each member of the group to have committed rape and prescribing mandatory life imprisonment or death.
simple_explanation: If a girl under 12 years of age is gang raped by a group of persons acting together or with a common intention, every person involved is deemed to have committed rape and must be punished with imprisonment for the remainder of their natural life along with fine, or with the death penalty.
objective: To provide the most severe punishment, including death, for gang rape of girls under twelve years of age.
legal_significance: Mandates life imprisonment (remainder of natural life) with fine, or death, for every participant in the gang rape of a child under twelve, with fine payable to the victim for medical expenses and rehabilitation.
essential_elements: The victim is a woman under twelve years of age.
The rape is committed by one or more persons constituting a group or acting in furtherance of a common intention.
Each such person is deemed to have committed the offence of rape.

mens_rea.description: Common intention shared among group members to commit rape, or participation as part of the group committing rape on a child under twelve.

normal_case.imprisonment: Imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life)

aggravated_case.imprisonment: Death

examples.scenario: A group of men gang rape a 10-year-old girl acting in furtherance of a common intention.
examples.result: Each man is deemed to have committed rape and is liable under Section 376DB with imprisonment for the remainder of natural life and fine, or death.

max_punishment: Death
ai_summary: Section 376DB IPC mandates imprisonment for the remainder of natural life and fine, or death, for every person involved in the gang rape of a girl under twelve years of age, with fine to be paid to the victim for medical expenses and rehabilitation.

title: Punishment for Repeat Offenders
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
bare_act_text: Whoever has been previously convicted of an offence punishable under section 376 or section 376A or section 376AB or section 376D or section 376DA or section 376DB, and is subsequently convicted of an offence punishable under any of the said sections shall be punished with imprisonment for life which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person's natural life, or with death.
meaning: Section 376E IPC prescribes mandatory life imprisonment or death for a person who, having been previously convicted under specified rape-related sections, is subsequently convicted again under any of those sections.
simple_explanation: If a person has already been convicted once for rape, gang rape, or related aggravated offences under specified sections, and commits and is convicted of any such offence again, they must be punished with imprisonment for the remainder of their natural life, or with death.
objective: To deter repeat sexual offenders by imposing the harshest possible punishment on subsequent conviction for specified rape-related offences.
legal_significance: Introduces a 'repeat offender' enhancement that removes sentencing discretion below life imprisonment, treating recidivism in sexual offences as warranting the severest punishment including death.
essential_elements: The accused has a prior conviction under Section 376, 376A, 376AB, 376D, 376DA, or 376DB.
The accused is subsequently convicted of an offence punishable under any of these same sections.

mens_rea.description: Mens rea as required for the underlying offence (rape or gang rape) for which the subsequent conviction is recorded; the section itself operates as a sentencing enhancement based on prior conviction.

normal_case.imprisonment: Imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life)

aggravated_case.imprisonment: Death

examples.scenario: A, previously convicted under Section 376 for rape, is subsequently convicted again under Section 376D for gang rape.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 376E and shall be punished with imprisonment for the remainder of his natural life, or with death.

max_punishment: Death
ai_summary: Section 376E IPC mandates imprisonment for the remainder of natural life or death for a person previously convicted under specified rape/gang rape provisions who is subsequently convicted again under any of those sections.

title: Unnatural Offences
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
bare_act_text: Whoever voluntarily has carnal intercourse against the order of nature with any man, woman or animal, shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine. Explanation.—Penetration is sufficient to constitute the carnal intercourse necessary to the offence described in this section.
meaning: Section 377 IPC punishes voluntary carnal intercourse against the order of nature with any man, woman, or animal.
simple_explanation: This section criminalizes certain forms of sexual acts considered 'against the order of nature' involving a man, woman, or animal, with penetration alone being sufficient to constitute the offence.
objective: To penalize specified non-consensual or unnatural sexual acts, including bestiality, with the offence historically also covering certain consensual acts (subject to later constitutional interpretation regarding consenting adults).
legal_significance: This section's application has been significantly narrowed by judicial interpretation (notably the Supreme Court's reading down of the provision insofar as it criminalized consensual sexual conduct between adults), and now primarily applies to non-consensual acts and bestiality.
essential_elements: The accused voluntarily has carnal intercourse.
Such intercourse is against the order of nature.
The act is committed with a man, woman, or animal.
Penetration is sufficient to constitute the offence.

mens_rea.description: Voluntary and intentional act of carnal intercourse against the order of nature.

normal_case.imprisonment: Imprisonment for life, or imprisonment of either description up to 10 years

examples.scenario: A voluntarily commits an act of bestiality with an animal.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 377 with imprisonment for life or up to 10 years, and fine.

max_punishment: Imprisonment for life, or imprisonment up to 10 years, and fine
ai_summary: Section 377 IPC punishes voluntary carnal intercourse against the order of nature with a man, woman, or animal, with punishment of imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine; its scope has been narrowed by judicial pronouncements regarding consensual acts between adults.

title: Theft
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
bare_act_text: Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any movable property out of the possession of any person without that person's consent, moves that property in order to such taking, is said to commit theft. Explanation 1.—A thing so long as it is attached to the earth, not being movable property, is not the subject of theft; but it becomes capable of being the subject of theft as soon as it is severed from the earth. Explanation 2.—A moving effected by the same act which effects the severance may be a theft. Explanation 3.—A person is said to cause a thing to move by removing an obstacle which prevented it from moving or by separating it from any other thing, as well as by actually moving it. Explanation 4.—A person, who by any means causes an animal to move, is said to move that animal, and to move everything which, in consequence of the motion so caused, is moved by that animal. Explanation 5.—The consent mentioned in the definition may be express or implied, and may be given either by the person in possession, or by any person having for that purpose authority either express or implied.
meaning: Section 378 IPC defines theft as dishonestly taking movable property out of another's possession without consent by moving it with that intention.
simple_explanation: Theft occurs when someone, intending to dishonestly take movable property belonging to another person without their consent, moves that property in order to take it away.
objective: To define the offence of theft precisely, clarifying what constitutes movable property, possession, consent, and the act of 'moving' property for the purposes of theft.
legal_significance: Forms the foundational definition for theft and related property offences (Sections 379-382), establishing that the offence is complete upon the dishonest 'moving' of property, even before it is fully removed from the owner's premises.
essential_elements: Dishonest intention to take movable property.
The property is in the possession of another person.
The taking is without that person's consent.
The accused moves the property in order to effect such taking.

mens_rea.description: Dishonest intention to take the property out of the possession of another without consent.

examples.scenario: A cuts down a tree on Z's land intending to dishonestly take it without Z's consent; as soon as A severs the tree for this purpose, theft is committed.
examples.result: A has committed theft as defined under Section 378.

examples.scenario: A finds a ring belonging to Z on a table in Z's house and dishonestly removes it.
examples.result: A commits theft as the ring was in Z's possession.

max_punishment: Not applicable (definition section; punishment prescribed under Section 379)
ai_summary: Section 378 IPC defines theft as the dishonest moving of movable property out of another's possession without consent, with the offence being complete as soon as such moving occurs with dishonest intent.

title: Punishment for Theft
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
bare_act_text: Whoever commits theft shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
meaning: Section 379 IPC prescribes the punishment for the offence of theft as defined under Section 378.
simple_explanation: Anyone who commits theft can be punished with imprisonment of up to 3 years, or a fine, or both.
objective: To prescribe the general punishment for the basic offence of theft.
legal_significance: Provides the standard punishment for simple theft, which is enhanced under subsequent sections (380-382) for aggravated forms of theft such as theft in a dwelling house, by a clerk/servant, or with preparation for violence.
essential_elements: The accused has committed theft as defined under Section 378.

mens_rea.description: Dishonest intention to take movable property out of another's possession without consent, as required under Section 378.

examples.scenario: A dishonestly takes Z's bicycle without Z's consent.
examples.result: A is liable under Section 379 with imprisonment up to 3 years, or fine, or both.

max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years, or fine, or both
compoundable: No (compoundable with permission of court in some cases)
ai_summary: Section 379 IPC prescribes punishment for theft as imprisonment up to 3 years, or fine, or both.

title: Snatching (Gujarat State Amendment)
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
bare_act_text: (1) Whoever, with the intention to commit theft, suddenly or quickly or forcibly seizes or secures or grabs or takes away from any person or from his physical possession any moveable property, and makes or attempt to make escape with such property, is said to commit snatching. (2) Whoever attempts to commit snatching shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than five years but which may extend to ten years, and with fine which may extend to twenty-five thousand rupees. (3) Whoever commits snatching shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than seven years but which may extend to ten years, and with fine which may extend to twenty-five thousand rupees.
